name: Andrews School
description_short: Andrews School offers online medical coding career training with comprehensive instructor support and preparation for professional credentials. Programs cost $3,600 with interest-free payment plans.
description_long:
Andrews School is an online educational institution specializing in medical coding career training. The school positions itself as a provider of comprehensive training programs designed to prepare students not just to pass certification exams, but to develop job-ready medical coding skills at a professional level. Founded and directed by Linda Andrews (CMT, FAAMT), the school operates with a focus on instructor-supported learning and real-world credential preparation.

The school offers a single primary program: online medical career training focused on medical coding. The tuition cost is $3,600 total, with interest-free payment plans available to make the program accessible. Students can choose flexible payment options starting with a minimum down payment of $150 (at $95.83/month) or a recommended down payment of $900 (at $75/month). The training duration varies by enrollment status—full-time students typically complete the program in approximately 12 months, while part-time students generally finish within 18-24 months. Students are responsible for purchasing their own course materials separately.

Andrews School distinguishes itself through direct instructor support and an emphasis on teaching practical, job-level coding skills rather than exam-focused instruction. The school maintains an active community through a private Facebook forum where students, graduates, instructors, and staff engage directly. This peer and instructor network provides ongoing support throughout the training process. The school's positioning emphasizes that employers recognize the difference between exam-passing training and genuinely job-ready preparation.

As an educational institution rather than a financial services provider, Andrews School functions as a workforce development resource. The school's current miscategorization as a payday-alternative product suggests confusion in the original database. While the interest-free payment plans are consumer-friendly, the core offering is vocational training. Prospective students should note that course materials are an additional out-of-pocket expense beyond the $3,600 tuition, and the time commitment (12-24 months) represents a substantial investment relative to short-term financial products.
services: Online medical coding career training program
Flexible payment options (multiple down payment levels)
Private student community forum on Facebook
pros: Interest-free payment plans available—no APR or finance charges on tuition
Flexible payment options ($150 minimum or $900 recommended down payment) accommodate different budgets
Direct instructor support included, not automated or self-paced only
Active student community via private Facebook forum for peer learning and support
Job-focused curriculum designed to teach professional-level coding, not just exam preparation
Reasonable total cost at $3,600 compared to many vocational training programs
Flexible scheduling with both full-time (12 months) and part-time (18-24 months) options
cons: Course materials must be purchased separately—additional out-of-pocket costs beyond $3,600 tuition
Part-time completion time extends to 18-24 months, requiring significant long-term commitment
Limited transparency—about page returns 404 error, reducing available company background information
No accreditation information visible on website (regional accreditation, ACCSC, etc. not mentioned)
Single program offering limits options for students with different career interests
best_for: Career changers seeking medical coding credentials with flexible, affordable payment options
Part-time learners balancing education with current employment or family responsibilities
Students prioritizing real instructor interaction over self-paced online learning
Budget-conscious students who need interest-free financing for vocational training
diagnosis: Andrews School is best for career changers or students seeking affordable, instructor-supported medical coding training with flexible payment options. The main caveat is that this is an educational program (not a financial product), requiring 12-24 months to complete and additional materials costs beyond tuition—making it unsuitable for those seeking quick income solutions or short-term financial assistance.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
